title: Heatwave in France causes worrying rise in emergency hospital visits
subhead: According to the French public health agency, emergency care visits for heatwave-related illnesses have increased sharply since June 22. Healthcare professionals are warning of the danger of further heatwaves during the summer vacation, when fewer hospital beds are available.

Heatwave puts French workers in harsh conditions: 'We have to keep our sleeveless fleece vests on so we're recognizable'
subhead: As more than half of mainland France was placed under a red heat alert on Tuesday, employees and civil servants spoke to Le Monde about their working conditions, revealing a lack of heatwave preparedness among employers.

title: Two killed in Israeli strike in South Lebanon, Hezbollah denounces true 'violation'
subhead: Two people were killed in southern Lebanon on Wednesday in an Israeli drone strike that Hezbollah said was the latest breach of a ceasefire agreement between the two sides.

title: The unavoidable rise of a less American world
subhead: The war in the Middle East has sped up a realignment that was already underway, forcing the US's partners to rethink flexible alliances without creating overly strong dependencies.

title: 250 years of American independence: A groundbreaking Constitution for 'a more perfect union'
subhead: 'America 250' (3/13). Eleven years after the founding of the United States, there was still much left to invent. The Constitution, adopted in September 1787, offered an unprecedented balance between the three branches of government in order to achieve 'a more perfect union' of the people. The first president, George Washington, elected in 1789, remains revered to this day.

Living under Paris's zinc roofs in 40°C heat: 'One day, the people living there will look for those responsible'
subhead: As France faces extreme heat, many of these top-story spaces have become uninhabitable. Some residents have formed groups to call on their landlords, neighbors and local authorities to address their living conditions.

title: Serving a husband, God and America: Young female conservatives gather at San Antonio's Turning Point summit
subhead: In early June, around 3,000 women attended the Women's Leadership Summit in San Antonio, Texas. This annual event brings together a variety of conservative figures and influencers, relying on the extensive network of Turning Point USA, founded by right-wing activist Charlie Kirk, who was murdered in 2025. After convincing a majority of young men to vote for Trump in 2024, the organization now targets female voters from Gen Z.

title: Montréal gunman leaves behind manifesto inspired by the 'incel' movement
subhead: A shooting in the Canadian city on Monday, June 22, left three dead, including a police officer and the attacker. In a manifesto, the shooter expressed his hatred of women.
